headlines.snippet: US says it targeted Iranian missile and drone storage locations while IRGC said it hit US military sites in the region.

headlines.title: Who profits from Africa’s gold?

headlines.title: Fishermen capture the moment earthquake hits Venezuelan coast
headlines.snippet: Fishermen captured the moment twin earthquakes struck Venezuela’s La Guaira coastline on Wednesday. The tremors were felt significantly in the region.

headlines.title: Lebanon’s state-owned NNA reports Israeli bombing near border
headlines.snippet: Lebanon’s state-owned NNA reports Israeli bombing near border Israeli forces overnight bombed a place in the town of Markaba, located about 1.5km from the Israel-Lebanon border, the state-owned National News Agency (NNA) reported.

headlines.title: Video: Netanyahu says Israel will remain in southern Lebanon until Hezbollah ‘disarm’
headlines.snippet: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that Israel will remain in southern Lebanon until Hezbollah disarms. This statement follows a framework agreement between Israel and Lebanon, brokered by the United States.

headlines.title: Morning update
headlines.snippet: A fragile US-Iran ceasefire is under strain following US strikes on Iranian targets in response to an alleged drone attack on a commercial vessel. Iran retaliated by targeting US positions.

headlines.title: Pakistan reaffirms support for regional peace in call with Iran
headlines.snippet: Pakistan's foreign minister, Mohammad Ishaq Dar, reaffirmed Islamabad's commitment to regional peace during a call with Iran's foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi. Araghchi thanked Pakistan for its support in advancing the peace process.

headlines.title: Israel to reduce forces in southern Lebanon, army radio says
headlines.snippet: The Israeli military plans to reduce its forces in southern Lebanon and withdraw some combat brigades, according to Israeli Army Radio. This decision comes after a framework agreement was reached between Lebanon and Israel with US involvement.

headlines.snippet: US strikes on Iranian military sites followed an attack on a cargo ship, raising concerns about security in the Strait of Hormuz and threatening a fragile ceasefire.

headlines.title: France’s terrorism apology law faces test in pro-Palestine case
headlines.snippet: A Paris court has frozen a trial related to France's terrorism apology law, raising a constitutional question about civil party powers. Campaigners view this as a victory for the Palestine movement.

headlines.title: Ms Rachel says world has failed Palestinian children at briefing
headlines.snippet: Ms Rachel expressed emotion during a briefing on Palestinian children, apologizing to a young girl and urging world leaders to take action.

headlines.title: Shia Muslims mark their holiest day in war-battered Lebanon
headlines.snippet: Shia Muslims observed Ashura by marching through the ruins of Nabatieh, defying the destruction from Israel's war on south Lebanon.

headlines.title: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon-Israel deal, warns of civil war
headlines.snippet: Hezbollah has rejected the Lebanon-Israel framework agreement, warning that its implementation could lead to civil war. Netanyahu stated that troops would remain in Lebanon.
